english: kiss one's hand to; blow a kiss

etymology.description: 飛(飛) (fēi, "fly") + 吻(吻) (wěn, "kiss") = to send a kiss through the air by blowing
etymology.interpretation: The word 飛吻 (fēiwěn) represents the action of sending a kiss through the air, as if it is flying. This combines the sense of motion from 飛(飛) (fēi, "fly") with the affectionate gesture of 吻(吻) (wěn, "kiss"). Such a gesture is often used to express warmth or affection from a distance.

examples.chinese: 她给我送了一个飞吻(她給我送了一個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi wǒ sòng le yī gè fēiwěn.

examples.chinese: 在道别的时候(在道別的時候)，他给了我一个飞吻(他給了我一個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Zài dàobié de shíhòu, tā gěi le wǒ yī gè fēiwěn.
examples.english: When saying goodbye, he blew me a kiss.

examples.chinese: 小女孩朝她的偶像送了一个飞吻(小女孩朝她的偶像送了一個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo nǚhái cháo tā de ǒuxiàng sòng le yī gè fēiwěn.
examples.english: The little girl blew a kiss to her idol.
